title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Behavior Case: Pyinstaller Boundary
summary: The game depends on the current directory, omits fonts and sound, builds one executable on Linux and calls it cross-platform, lacks licenses, and is never installed fresh. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pyinstaller boundary increment in portable inspected game release candidate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Pyinstaller Boundary · Theory Pyinstaller Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 1614 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Pyinstaller Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to portable inspected game release candidate.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-packaging-assets-platforms-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-packaging-assets-platforms-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_packaging_assets_platforms_5(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, declared_assets: int = 12, packaged_assets: int = 12, licenses_recorded: bool = True, offline_launch: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, target_platforms, "package-domain"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "platform-gap"
    if packaged_assets != declared_assets:
        return False, packaged_assets, "asset-gap"
    if not licenses_recorded or not offline_launch:
        return False, packaged_assets, "release-input"
    assert packaged_assets == declared_assets
    return True, target_platforms, "portable-build"

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Repair the Pyinstaller Boundary defect · Theory Pyinstaller Bounda…, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 6491 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.

stimulus.title: Pyinstaller Boundary failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: PyInstaller cross-compiles Windows and macOS executables from Linux.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the pyinstaller boundary defect evidence boundary.

For repair the pyinstaller boundary defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Pyinstaller Boundary, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pyinstaller Boundary model · Theory Pyinstaller Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pyinstaller Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4872 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Pyinstaller Boundary model · Theory Pyinstaller Boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build with PyInstaller on each target OS, inspect analysis and contents, handle data and native libraries, and test one-folder before one-file.
